evals.prompt:
Add Auth0 authentication to an Express Node.js application using the express-oauth2-jwt-bearer SDK.

**Auth0 Credentials:**
- Domain: `dev-example.auth0.com`
- Client ID: `abc123def456ghi789jkl012`
- API Audience: `https://api.example.com`
evals.expected_output: Working Express Node.js API with Auth0 JWT validation, protected endpoints, and RBAC permissions
expectations: Configures JWT Bearer token validation middleware
Configures API audience for token validation
Has protected API endpoint with JWT middleware
Auth0 domain dev-example.auth0.com written to .env config file
API audience https://api.example.com written to .env config file
No login UI methods or callback URLs in stateless API
No client secret in API config (uses JWKS for validation)
Solution correctly configures Auth0 JWT Bearer token validation, RBAC permissions, and CORS in an Express Node.js API
Uses current SDK version (v1.7.4) in dependency declaration
Configures specific token validation options (clockTolerance, issuerBaseURL, or tokenSigningAlg)
Uses claim validation functions (requiredScopes, claimIncludes, claimCheck) for RBAC
Does not use deprecated express-jwt + jwks-rsa combo (uses current express-oauth2-jwt-bearer)
At least 2 skill-specific advanced patterns present (version, advanced validation options, RBAC, DPoP, CORS ordering)
